name: Proto-Cushitic (family node)
notes: STRUCTURAL ANCESTRY NODE (not a modelled G2P target). Proto-Cushitic is the reconstructed ancestor of the Cushitic branch of Afroasiatic; its Agaw (Central Cushitic) members are the classic substrate of Ethiopian Semitic (Amharic, Tigrinya). It exists here only so the ancestry / phylogenetic-distance metrics of the modern varieties that reference it have a node to resolve to; NO phonology is claimed or reconstructed and the minimal grapheme set is a placeholder (afa.json / trk.json stub pattern).

sources.author: Appleyard, D.
sources.title: A Comparative Dictionary of the Agaw Languages
sources.notes: Reference for the Cushitic substrate of Ethiopian Semitic (structural node only).
